title: Renewables remain cheapest, but their LCOE is rising: Lazard
snippet: Utility-scale solar’s levelized cost of electricity ranges between $40/MWh and $98/MWh, while combined cycle gas ranges between $51/MWh and $129/MWh, said a report from Lazard.
ai_summary: Lazard's LCOE analysis shows renewables remain cost-competitive but with rising costs, relevant to tem's positioning in the renewable energy supply market and understanding generator economics.
ai_reasoning: General industry trend piece on renewable economics and LCOE trends. Provides useful market context for understanding cost dynamics that affect both generators and I&C customers, but no direct business impact or named mention. Background reading suitable for market intelligence.
